# NEXO Brain

> Open-source cognitive runtime with a shared brain for Claude Code, Codex, Claude Desktop, and other MCP clients (v7.20.14). NEXO Desktop is a separate closed-source companion app distributed at nexo-desktop.com; its source does not ship in this repo. Mentions of "NEXO Desktop" in changelog entries describe coordinated client releases that consume the Brain CLI / MCP contract. For the commercial Desktop product, contact info@wazion.com.

NEXO Brain gives AI agents persistent memory using the Atkinson-Shiffrin cognitive architecture. It adds a local shared brain across supported clients, configurable terminal and automation backends, recovery-aware background jobs, startup preflight, runtime self-healing, smarter retrieval defaults, long-horizon overnight analysis, and 150+ MCP tools.

v7.20.14: patch release. Brain protects Local Memory during update/recovery paths, rotates runtime backup families to the latest 5 entries, keeps first-indexing status stable, and exposes bounded indexing speed profiles for Desktop.
v7.20.13: patch release. Brain recovery now pauses all known DB writers before restoring `nexo.db`, and Doctor can repair the zero-byte/locked database state that made Desktop Local Memory show zero files.
v7.20.12: patch release. Local Context keeps the first index pass separate from live change tracking, persists the current indexing start time, returns compact bounded local evidence for agents, and installs the Windows host scheduler for WSL indexing after reboot.
v7.20.11: patch release. Local Context now starts from real system volume roots plus mounted/removable/network volumes, skips system/cache/app/product artifacts, and injects local evidence excerpts automatically into heartbeat, task-open and pre-action context.
v7.20.10: patch release. Local Context manual refreshes now reconcile automatic roots every time, so mounted disks and upgraded default roots are picked up immediately from Desktop's check-now path.
v7.20.9: patch release. Local Context now scans automatic roots at full operational depth, falls back to managed crontab when Linux/WSL systemd user timers fail, passes Windows AppData email roots into WSL, blocks Google API keys before HTML cleaning, and keeps Outlook Mac proprietary files inventory-only.
v7.20.8: patch release. Local Context recognises Windows Mail package roots and Outlook Mac profile roots as bounded local-email sources instead of rejecting them as generic AppData / Group Containers.
v7.20.7: patch release. Local Context email-root bootstrap is deterministic across CI, WSL and migrated profiles while preserving macOS Mail.app, Windows Outlook, Thunderbird and NEXO email coverage.
v7.20.6: patch release. Local Context now ranks entity matches at chunk level, keeps old entity-matched assets eligible, adds safe local email roots for macOS/Windows/Linux, extracts `.eml`, `.emlx`, `.msg` and NEXO email DB continuity, and exposes local graph relations in pre-action context.
v7.20.5: patch release. Local Context status now reports elapsed indexing time and a defensive ETA while background jobs remain pending.
v7.20.4: patch release. Local Context now blocks private dotfiles, hidden project folders and secret-bearing content before chunks, embeddings, graph relations or agent context are created.
v7.20.3: patch release. Installer DMG volumes are no longer added as local-memory roots, removed roots purge stale payloads, and doctor can repair removed-root residue.
v7.20.2: patch release. Local Context now requeues retryable failed jobs, recovers expired running leases, reports real macOS/Windows service health, records scan errors and preserves Windows drive roots.
v7.20.1: patch release. Local Context service cycles now recover from orphaned locks and mixed-version `run_once()` parameter drift, so the background index keeps advancing instead of silently getting stuck.
v7.20.0: minor release. Local Context now reconciles known files and folders on every service cycle, so created, modified, deleted and newly excluded local files are reflected automatically between full scans. It also returns graph relations with local evidence and clears stale macOS Full Disk Access prompts after real verification.
v7.19.0: minor release. Bundle-managed installations (NEXO Desktop `brain-bundle/`) can now pin Brain to the host application release cycle via `NEXO_BRAIN_AUTO_UPDATE=false`, and the server auto-exits with code 75 on fingerprint mismatch so MCP clients respawn the server with the new code instead of leaving stale `server.py` processes alive.
v7.18.1: patch release. Packaged Brain runtimes now include the `local_context` package during install/update, fixing `ModuleNotFoundError` and zero-file Desktop Local Memory status after the 7.18.0 Local Context release. The local-index service also rechecks automatic roots on every cycle so newly mounted volumes are picked up without manual setup.
v7.18.0: minor release. Brain adds the Local Context Layer: a local-only background memory index with checkpoints, extraction, graph links, embeddings, MCP/CLI controls, and pre-action evidence for agents.
v7.17.8: patch release. standalone `nexo chat` now surfaces macOS Full Disk Access guidance, and Brain clears stale permission state after a live probe succeeds.
v7.17.7: patch release. macOS `tcc-approve` privacy denials now write a guided Full Disk Access permission state instead of surfacing as unexplained cron failures.
v7.17.5: patch release. `nexo --version --json` now returns fast machine-readable update status for Desktop (`installed`, `latest`, `hasUpdate`, `unknown`, `latestSource`) while `nexo --version` keeps the legacy first line and adds a compact latest/installed status line for older fallbacks.
v7.17.4: corrective patch release. Automation runners now keep full NEXO discipline for real background agents while strict JSON children stay clean. Guardian metrics count the real `injection` event, and runtime doctor surfaces cron/caller coverage drift instead of hiding blind spots.
v7.17.3: corrective patch release. Standalone Brain install/update no longer aborts when the Desktop-only `qwen3-0.6b-q4-local-presence` model is not bundled or already cached locally. The manifest now marks that GGUF optional, and strict warmup still fails only when required Brain models fail.
v7.17.2: patch release. email-monitor now guards its suffixed `/tmp/nexo-*` reply/quote/thread temp buffers before writing, morning-agent marks interrupted or stale briefing claims failed before retrying, and Codex managed config migrates from legacy `[features].codex_hooks` to `[features].hooks`.
v7.17.1: patch release. _extract_claude_telemetry now handles the Claude CLI 2.1+ direct-JSON response shape (no `result` wrapper). Pre-7.17.1 callers using output_format=json + bare_mode received an empty stdout because the parser assumed the classic wrapper; the agent's actual JSON was already persisted in automation_runs but never reached the caller. This fix unblocks the daily morning-agent briefing (178 "invalid JSON output" failures observed on 2026-05-10 vs 75 successful telemetry rows for the same runs).
v7.17.0: minor release. The headless runner pre-emptive guard becomes advisory: it surfaces learnings and schemas to the agent up front and logs to guard_checks for observability, but never returns blocked=True. The PreToolUse hook is the authoritative gate at write time. This closes the family of bugs where heuristic path matches in the prompt aborted email-monitor, followup-runner, Deep Sleep synth, postmortem-consolidation, and hn-karma. Also rolls in the directory-path hardening planned for 7.16.4: handle_guard_check now skips Path.is_dir() entries and the path extractor strips trailing slashes.
v7.16.3: patch release. Pre-emptive runner guard stops duplicating the runtime-core write protection. The PreToolUse hook already blocks actual writes on core paths with severity error; the runner now opts out of that rule, removing the entire family of false positives where merely mentioning a core path in the prompt aborted the session.
v7.16.2: patch release. Stops the pre-emptive runner guard from aborting email-monitor sessions over the reply tool: paths immediately following a known interpreter (python3, node, bash, etc.) are recognised as subprocess invocations and excluded from the runtime-core blocking rule.
v7.16.0: minor release. Adds Memory Observations v2: evidence-backed event capture, derived observations, update-safe backfill, MCP retrieval, dashboard visibility, and stricter refusal when memory lacks evidence.
v7.15.2: patch release. Codex bootstrap diagnostics now treat normal startup context reads of calibration and project-atlas files as healthy bootstrap activity instead of conditioned-file drift.
v7.15.1: patch release. Drains larger self-audit clusters, bounds hook_runs history with migration and retention cleanup, ignores normal Codex startup-context calibration and project-atlas reads, routes email-monitor effort by message complexity, and locks morning briefings by local date and recipient.
v7.15.0: minor release. Unifies sent-email continuity across send paths, switches recall to multilingual embeddings, forces tagged learnings into context, adds hard email loop guards, exposes learning creation dates, protects headless runners with guard checks, and writes AUTO-N burst postmortems.
v7.13.9: patch release. Desktop-managed installs now move aside an existing managed `.venv` if it was created with unsupported Python <3.10, then recreate it with the supported Python prepared by Desktop.
v7.13.8: patch release. Desktop-managed fresh installs reject Python <3.10 before dependency installation, honor the prepared Desktop Python interpreter, and fail clearly instead of surfacing fastmcp resolution noise from Apple Python 3.9.
v7.13.7: patch release. Adds authenticated official protocol-card tools (`nexo_card_catalog`, `nexo_card_get`, `nexo_card_match`) that fetch task protocols from the NEXO Desktop backend at runtime. The open-source Brain package does not embed the private protocol corpus.
v7.13.6: patch release. Codex hook sync now renders the managed PreToolUse guard with native Windows `cmd.exe` syntax for shell/exec_command calls while keeping the existing POSIX command on macOS/Linux. This closes the native Windows hook parity risk without changing Desktop installation.
v7.13.5: corrective release. D.5 correction-learning enforcement is durable, doctor `--fix` explicitly repairs orphan personal schedule metadata, G5 warns on protected `com.nexo.*.plist` unload/remove flows with the three-layer recovery path, Codex sync installs a managed PreToolUse guard for shell/exec_command calls, and `installation_live.codex_protocol_compliance` fails if that live hook is missing or if Codex drift exceeds the allowed threshold.
v7.13.3: unified release. Doctor repairs orphan personal script metadata and skips historical runtime snapshots, `nexo update` prunes snapshots older than two back, protocol compliance auto-opens edit tasks and records change-log visibility, stale tasks auto-close as partial, headless automation uses bounded timeouts, Guardian false positives are tightened, and Codex CLI config/default checks are release-gated.
v7.12.15: patch release. Packaged ``nexo update`` now runs safe same-version maintenance for refreshed Desktop bundles, Deep Sleep cleans process locks on normal and interrupted exits, sent replies are written to durable continuity, and personal script reconcile reports LaunchAgent managed-marker drift.
v7.12.14: patch release. Dashboard followup execution now uses the selected terminal client from NEXO preferences and opens the right native terminal path on macOS, Windows via WSL, and Linux. ``POST /api/followups/{id}/run`` no longer returns a macOS-only 501 on supported non-Darwin hosts.
v7.12.2: patch release. Legacy automation routing no longer bypasses the resonance engine: ``src/agent_runner.py`` stops letting ``task_profile`` prefill backend/model/reasoning_effort, ``src/client_preferences.py`` normalizes persisted ``automation_task_profiles`` back to empty routing fields, ``src/scripts/nexo-email-monitor.py`` stops passing or persisting its old private automation profile, and ``src/scripts/nexo_personal_automation.py`` stops injecting ``resolve_user_model()`` into short text runs. ``src/auto_update.py`` also scrubs the retired email routing key from legacy runtime config during update so existing installs converge on the same caller-driven path as Deep Sleep and morning-agent.
v7.12.0: minor release. Adds ``nexo support-snapshot`` as a generic local runtime diagnostics bundle and completes the silent-reminder hardening on the live Protocol Enforcer path. ``src/support_snapshot.py`` collects version/platform data, runtime path presence, health output, and recent event/operation tails behind ``nexo support-snapshot [--include-doctor]``. In parallel, ``tool-enforcement-map.json`` now upgrades startup / smart-startup / heartbeat / reminders / diary / stop / task-close / compaction prompts to the explicit turn-wide silence contract, and ``src/enforcement_engine.py`` defensively upgrades any legacy ``Do not produce visible text`` reminder before enqueueing it. Result: background reminders stop leaking orphan assistant continuations into Desktop when there is no fresh user message.
v7.11.7: patch release. ``src/doctor/providers/runtime.py`` now stops treating resolved or intentional state as live runtime failure: disabled ``evolution`` no longer trips cron freshness, historical conditioned-file drift heals once open debt is zero, cancelled tasks no longer lower ``change_log`` coverage, successful headless zero-usage zero-cost runs are excluded from automation telemetry denominators, and recent in-flight cron rows stay fresh instead of showing stale. ``src/scripts/runner-health-check.py`` now treats supervisor ``SIGTERM 143`` reloads as benign and supports both tuple and row-shaped SQLite reads, removing false runner-health warnings from live installs. Validation: ``104`` targeted tests across doctor and runner-health, plus source-runtime doctor smoke returning ``overall_status: healthy``.
v7.11.6: patch release. ``src/hook_guardrails.py`` now filters more false-positive slash fragments before they become protocol debt: ``|``, ``=``, and ``;`` are treated as path-artifact markers, date-like tokens such as ``/04/2026`` are rejected, and non-extension multi-segment slash tokens must exist on disk before they count as real paths. The same patch also keeps strict writes fail-closed while downgrading ``strict_protocol_write_without_task`` debt to ``warn`` when the session has a heartbeat in the last 5 minutes, so dashboards stop mixing active-session drift with fully untracked writes. ``src/scripts/deep-sleep/extract.py`` now validates the live extraction contract (``session_id``, ``findings``, and structured ``protocol_summary``) before declaring success; structurally degraded JSON becomes deterministic ``json_schema`` failure with a ``debug-extract-*-json_schema.txt`` artifact instead of silently feeding partial payloads into synthesis. Validation: ``50`` targeted tests across hook guardrails and Deep Sleep extraction.
v7.11.5: patch release. Desktop-managed installs now block the standalone dashboard at the same product-mode layer as evolution. ``src/product_mode.py`` expands ``DESKTOP_DISABLED_FEATURES`` to include ``dashboard``, and ``is_cron_blocked()`` now checks membership in that set instead of a hard-coded evolution-only branch. Before this, ``nexo doctor --plane installation_live`` correctly marked a standalone ``com.nexo.dashboard.plist`` as drift when Desktop managed the product surface, but cron sync/watchdog still expected ``dashboard`` as a core keepalive service because product-mode only blocked ``evolution``. Repairing the drift made the watchdog fail; refreshing the runtime reinstalled the dashboard to satisfy the watchdog and reintroduced the drift. v7.11.5 makes installation_live, cron sync, and watchdog agree: on Desktop-managed installs the standalone dashboard is blocked, not installed, and not monitored. Validation: ``125`` targeted tests across product-mode, cron sync, and doctor, plus the full pre-release wrapper still green (``2321 passed, 2 skipped, 1 xfailed, 4 xpassed``).
v7.11.4: patch release. Closes the remaining installed-runtime parity gaps that source checkouts were no longer hitting. Root JSON runtime contracts (including ``local_model_manifest.json`` and other ``*_manifest.json`` / ``*_defaults.json`` / ``*_tiers.json`` files) now ship into packaged runtimes through both ``bin/nexo-brain.js`` and ``src/auto_update.py`` instead of being silently omitted from ``~/.nexo/core``. Install/update cron sync is manifest-driven again via ``syncCoreProcessesFromManifest(...)`` on fresh install, same-version refresh, and versioned migration/update before falling back to the legacy JS allowlist path, so crons declared in ``src/crons/manifest.json`` are the ones actually installed in packaged runtimes. ``runner-health-check`` is now registered in the cron manifest, its dead followup-activity query is fixed, doctor/runtime health reads the report, and dashboard exposes both ``runner-health-report.json`` and ``morning-briefing-latest.md`` instead of leaving them as write-only artifacts. ``nexo-watchdog.sh`` now treats ``run_once_on_wake`` as a catchup-style recovery policy and retries failed crons immediately instead of waiting for stale-age healing; its stuck-wrapper PID match is also scoped to the current runtime wrapper path so one install cannot misidentify another install's wrapper that happens to share the same ``cron_id``. Validation: ``117`` targeted tests across cron recovery, packaged update/runtime, dashboard, local models, and runtime update contracts.
v7.11.3: patch release. Root-cause fix for the ``mcp_restart_required`` lockup that v7.11.2 only masked at the enforcer layer. ``_FINGERPRINT_EXCLUDE_DIRS`` in ``src/runtime_versioning.py`` was missing ``"versions"``, so ``compute_mcp_runtime_fingerprint()`` walked into ``core/versions/<old>/**.py`` whenever it was called against the live runtime root. ``installed_runtime_fingerprint()`` (which resolves through ``active_runtime_root()`` → ``core/versions/<active>/``) returned a clean per-snapshot hash, while ``prime_process_fingerprint()`` (which starts from ``Path(__file__).resolve().parent`` → live ``core/``) accumulated every retained snapshot under ``core/versions/``. The two fingerprints never matched after the second-ever ``nexo update`` on a host. Every update wrote ``mcp-restart-required.json`` and the marker could never be cleared by ``_ack_current_client_if_restarted()`` because the ``installed_fp != process_fp`` test always returned ``True``. Every non-allowlisted MCP tool (``nexo_reminders``, ``nexo_smart_startup``, ``nexo_guard_check``, ``nexo_task_open``, …) returned ``{"error": "mcp_restart_required", "reason": "fingerprint_mismatch"}`` indefinitely, even after the operator restarted the client (the new client connected to the same server with the same cached ``PROCESS_FINGERPRINT``). v7.11.2 silenced the symptom with the enforcer gate (``HeadlessEnforcer._mcp_restart_pending``) but the marker itself never got cleared, so user-driven tool calls kept failing across sessions. Adding ``"versions"`` to ``_FINGERPRINT_EXCLUDE_DIRS`` restores parity: both fingerprint computations now hash the same set of files regardless of which entry path the caller starts from. 1 new regression test in ``tests/test_runtime_fingerprint.py`` (``test_fingerprint_ignores_versions_subtree``) seeds three snapshot directories under ``versions/`` and asserts the fingerprint does not shift. The two existing exclude-dir tests now also cover ``"versions"``. All 21 tests in ``tests/test_runtime_fingerprint.py`` stay green.

v7.11.2: patch release. Two reliability fixes in the same family ("components ignoring signals they should respect"). (1) Adds a ``STUCK CRON REAPER`` to ``src/scripts/nexo-watchdog.sh`` that closes the gap left by the v5.8.1 in-flight detection. The v5.8.1 fix made the watchdog leave running jobs alone (it had been ``kickstart -k``-ing ``deep-sleep`` mid-flight 2026-04-14..17). The same restraint silently let truly hung wrappers — e.g. headless ``claude --bare`` blocked on an MCP that flagged ``mcp_restart_required`` after a brain update — block their own next tick for days. ``morning-agent``, ``followup-runner`` and ``orchestrator-v2`` went silent 2026-04-24..27 because a single zombi wrapper held the slot indefinitely and the watchdog only logged WARN. The new ``run_stuck_reaper()`` sweep runs at the top of every watchdog tick, before the per-monitor loop. It reads every ``cron_runs`` row with ``ended_at IS NULL`` and compares its age to ``stuck_after_seconds`` (per-cron from ``src/crons/manifest.json``, fallback ``STUCK_DEFAULT_SECONDS=43200`` = 12h global). Live wrapper → ``SIGTERM`` to the wrapper PID (``pgrep -f "nexo-cron-wrapper.sh CRON_ID "``); the wrapper's existing trap forwards to the child and runs ``finalize_row``, closing the row at ``exit_code=143``. After 10s grace, any survivor (wrapper + descendants) gets ``SIGKILL`` via ``pkill -KILL -P``. Orphan zombi row (wrapper PID gone, row still NULL) → cleaned in-band: ``ended_at=now``, ``exit_code=137``, ``summary='stuck row reaped by watchdog: wrapper PID gone'``. ``cron_id='watchdog'`` is hard-coded into ``STUCK_REAPER_SKIP`` — never reap ourselves mid-tick. Per-cron overrides for known long-runners: ``deep-sleep: 28800`` (8h), ``sleep: 14400`` (4h), ``evolution: 14400`` (4h, weekly heavy run). Short-runners get tighter bounds: ``morning-agent: 1800``, ``followup-runner: 1800``, ``email-monitor: 600``. New observability counter ``TOTAL_REAPED`` in three places: ``watchdog-status.json`` (``summary.reaped``), human report header (``REAPED:``), final log line (``REAPED=N``). 6 new tests in ``tests/test_watchdog_stuck_reaper.py``: fresh in-flight row left alone (v5.8.1 regression guard), per-cron threshold respected (``deep-sleep`` 8h not reaped at 4h), orphan zombi row cleaned in-band with ``exit_code=137``, real wrapper killed via SIGTERM with the trap closing the row at ``exit_code=143``, ``cron_id='watchdog'`` never reaped, default 12h threshold applied to crons not in manifest. The 3 existing watchdog tests stay green. (2) The Guardian/Enforcer (``HeadlessEnforcer`` in ``src/enforcement_engine.py``) periodically injects ``<system-reminder>`` blocks asking the agent to call ``nexo_*`` tools (``heartbeat``, ``session_diary_write``, ``smart_startup``, ``guard_check``, etc). When the MCP server has the ``~/.nexo/runtime/operations/mcp-restart-required.json`` marker on disk (written by ``plugins/update.py`` after a ``nexo update`` that actually changes runtime ``.py`` bytes — see v7.11.0 fingerprint gating), every one of those reminders triggered a tool call that immediately failed with ``mcp_restart_required``. The agent burned cycles on guaranteed no-ops until the operator restarted the client. v7.11.2 adds a gate at the top of ``_enqueue()``: if the prompt mentions ``nexo_`` and the marker file exists, skip + log ``SKIP: ... mcp_restart_required marker present``. Reminders that don't reference ``nexo_*`` (R23 deploy guards, R25 nora/maria read-only, etc.) still fire — they don't depend on the MCP being live. New helpers: ``HeadlessEnforcer._mcp_restart_marker_path()`` (resolves canonical F0.6 location with pre-F0.6 fallback) and ``HeadlessEnforcer._mcp_restart_pending()`` (cached per-instance with 30s TTL so we don't stat the marker on every ``_enqueue`` call). Conservative: any path/IO error in the resolver returns ``False`` so the gate never blocks legitimate enforcement. 6 new tests in ``tests/test_enforcer_restart_required_gate.py``: nexo_* prompt enqueues normally with no marker, nexo_* prompt skipped with marker present, non-nexo_* prompt still enqueues with marker, 30s TTL cache behavior, pre-F0.6 legacy ``operations/`` path detected, missing ``NEXO_HOME`` returns ``False`` instead of raising. The 52 existing enforcer tests stay green.
v7.11.1: patch release. Fingerprint cache by ``(file_count, size_total, max_mtime)`` signature in ``~/.nexo/runtime/operations/fingerprint-cache.json``. ``prime_process_fingerprint()`` (called once at MCP server startup) and ``installed_runtime_fingerprint()`` (hot path: runs on every tool call via ``resolve_restart_required``) now consult the cache before re-reading the 263 ``.py`` files of the runtime tree. When the on-disk signature still matches, the cached digest is returned without I/O. Local benchmark on the live ``src/`` tree: ~40ms cold → ~3.7ms warm (≈11x speedup). Across daily MCP startups (Claude Code, Codex, headless followup-runner, deep-sleep, crons): ~10-20s saved per day. New keyword-only parameter ``compute_mcp_runtime_fingerprint(src_dir, *, use_cache=False)`` — default stays ``False`` so ``plugins/update.py`` keeps seeing ground truth around ``git pull`` / ``npm update``. Hot callers opt in. Cache miss is always safe: corrupt JSON, signature drift, missing file → fall through to the full hash and rewrite the cache atomically. 6 new tests in ``tests/test_runtime_fingerprint.py`` covering cache-hit-skips-reads (spy on ``Path.read_bytes``), cache miss on file change, cache miss on different src_dir, corrupt-cache self-repair, default-off-for-update-flow, prime warms the on-disk cache.
v7.11.0: minor release. Introduces a runtime fingerprint that gates ``mcp-restart-required.json``. Before this release every ``nexo update`` that bumped ``version.json`` forced every connected MCP client (Claude Code, Codex, Claude Desktop) to restart its session, even when the release was byte-identical to the running code at the Python level (cf. v7.10.1, a README-only release that still triggered a forced restart for everyone). The runtime now computes a sha256 over every ``.py`` file under ``src/`` that the live server can import (excluding ``scripts/``, ``tests/``, ``migrations/``, ``crons/``, ``__pycache__/``, ``node_modules/``, ``.git/``) and only writes the marker when that fingerprint changes between the pre-update and post-update trees. README-only / blog-only / changelog-only releases now skip the marker entirely; ``nexo update`` reports ``MCP source unchanged (no .py byte changed) — no restart needed.``. New helpers in ``src/runtime_versioning.py``: ``compute_mcp_runtime_fingerprint``, ``installed_runtime_fingerprint``, ``prime_process_fingerprint``, ``installed_force_restart_flag`` and module-level ``PROCESS_FINGERPRINT`` cache primed at server startup. ``resolve_restart_required`` now uses fingerprint match as the primary signal and falls back to legacy version-string mismatch only when the fingerprint cannot be computed. ``mcp-restart-required.json`` schema bumped to v2 with optional ``from_fingerprint`` / ``to_fingerprint``; backwards-compatible reader. Conservative fallback honored (#186): missing fingerprint on either side = treat as code-changing and force restart. Explicit opt-in escape hatch via ``"force_restart": true`` in ``version.json`` for releases whose behavior change can't be detected from ``.py`` bytes (e.g. wire format of a config file read via ``json.load``); reason becomes ``brain_update_force``. ``nexo mcp-status --json`` now exposes ``installed_fingerprint``, ``process_fingerprint``, ``fingerprint_match``. 14 new tests in ``tests/test_runtime_fingerprint.py`` plus 4 new packaged-update tests. Full write-up in ``docs/runtime-fingerprint.md``.
v7.10.1: patch release. Removes a residual "Custom LLM endpoint (advanced)" section in ``README.md`` (around line 1096) that still documented the ``llm_endpoint.json`` / ``auth_provider.json`` override path as a current feature, including the ``Idempotency-Key`` proxy semantics and a pointer to the now-deleted ``docs/api/override-files.md``. The 7.10.0 code revert was complete; the README cleanup landed in 7.10.1. Repo-wide grep is now 0-hits in the published npm tarball as well, not just in the source tree.
v7.10.0: minor release. **Removes the LLM proxy override path introduced in 7.9.28 → 7.9.34.** Background: 7.9.28 added two opt-in files at ``~/.nexo/config/llm_endpoint.json`` and ``~/.nexo/config/auth_provider.json`` that let a third-party orchestrator (NEXO Desktop) redirect every Anthropic SDK call from Brain to a custom proxy and resolve the bearer via a local helper, with concrete model names translated to wire aliases (``nexo-max``, ``nexo-high``, ``nexo-medium``, ``nexo-low``, ``nexo-mini``) and an ``Idempotency-Key`` per request. NEXO Desktop's commercial model has changed: Desktop is now a wrapper over the user's own Claude Code subscription (Max / Pro), with a separate Desktop licence. Brain calls go directly to ``api.anthropic.com`` using the user's existing OAuth (the one stored under ``~/.claude/`` and consumed by Claude Code spawns) or a plain ``ANTHROPIC_API_KEY``. There is no NEXO bearer, no NEXO proxy, no NEXO credit accounting. Removed: every override symbol from ``src/call_model_raw.py`` (override-mode block, ``_CONCRETE_TO_ALIAS``, ``is_override_mode``, ``resolve_api_base_url``, ``resolve_auth_token``, ``_resolve_auth_provider_token``, ``_resolve_override_alias``, the ``idempotency_key`` parameter, the ``Idempotency-Key`` header injection, ``NEXO_RAW_ANTHROPIC``); the entire ``_apply_llm_endpoint_override`` helper from ``src/agent_runner.py`` and every call site; ``docs/api/override-files.md``; ``tests/test_call_model_raw_overrides*.py`` and ``tests/test_agent_runner_override_env.py``. The two opt-in files on disk (if any operator has them) are simply ignored from this release forward.
v7.9.34: patch release. Two fixes. First, ``_parse_email_headers`` in the email monitor was dropping any email whose RFC822 headers came back as ``email.header.Header`` instances (Q-encoded utf-8 / quoted-printable). ``msg.get("Message-ID").strip()`` raised ``TypeError: 'Header' object is not subscriptable``, the exception was swallowed at DEBUG, and the email vanished silently — operators only noticed because Nero stopped replying. Every ``msg.get(...)`` now goes through ``_decode_header`` (decodes Q-encoding AND coerces to ``str``), and the failure log is lifted to WARNING. Second, the PreToolUse Guardian gate used to emit JSON ``permissionDecision: deny`` but exit 0 — terminal Claude Code occasionally proceeded with the next tool anyway. Hard blocks now also write the structured Guardian reason to stderr and exit 2, the documented PreToolUse blocking exit. Belt-and-suspenders enforcement: model receives the Guardian reason through both channels and self-corrects instead of blindly retrying. 5 new parser tests + 2 hardened hook tests.
v7.9.33: patch release. Adds ``usedforsecurity=False`` to the SHA-1 call introduced in 7.9.32 that derives a filesystem-safe checkpoint filename from the email's Message-ID. Bandit B324 had failed the 7.9.32 publish workflow on a non-security SHA-1 usage. ``nexo-brain@7.9.33`` is the first release that carries the 7.9.32 email-recovery checkpoints.
v7.9.32: patch release. Hardens the email monitor's recovery against cascades of Brain releases. The ``_recover_unreplied_processed`` periodic sweep now uses a 7-day lookback (was 24h) so an email that falls between releases cannot age out of the recovery window before the next sweep notices. Each failed worker run persists a per-email checkpoint at ``~/.nexo/nexo-email/checkpoints/`` capturing files touched, last assistant narration, and last error. Retry attempts inject that context into the next prompt so a long task (drafting a presentation, multi-step analysis) continues from where the previous attempt died instead of restarting from scratch. Stale checkpoints are auto-pruned after 7 days. 15 new unit tests cover the helpers.
v7.9.31: patch release. Fixes a wire-level bug where ``call_model_raw`` was sending ``stop_sequences=["\n", ".", " "]`` by default, which the current Anthropic Messages API rejects with HTTP 400 ``each stop sequence must contain non-whitespace``. The default is now ``None`` (no ``stop_sequences`` field sent on the wire) since ``max_tokens=3`` already caps the yes/no classifier output. A local guard rejects whitespace-only caller values up front so the error surfaces where the caller is, not as a remote 400. Also removes an internal design document that did not belong in the open-source distribution. Detected during real wire smoke; covered by two new e2e tests and three new unit tests.
v7.9.30: patch release. Hotfix over v7.9.29: ``src/agent_runner.py`` was missing ``import sys`` even though the new ``_apply_llm_endpoint_override`` helper wrote to ``sys.stderr`` when override mode lacked an auth provider. Ruff F821 caught the undefined-name reference in CI's ``tests/test_fase4_lint_baseline.py::TestRuffPasses::test_ruff_check_src_returns_zero``, which failed the 7.9.29 publish workflow before any npm artifact shipped. v7.9.30 adds the missing import; the v7.9.29 tag is preserved on the repo for traceability but ``nexo-brain@7.9.30`` is the first npm release that carries the 7.9.29 override-path hardening.
v7.9.29: patch release. Hardens the optional LLM endpoint and auth provider override path introduced in 7.9.28. Override mode now passes the bearer to the Anthropic SDK as `auth_token` so it serialises into `Authorization: Bearer ...`; 7.9.28 sent it via `api_key` which produced `X-Api-Key` and any compatible proxy rejected every request with 401. The Brain config directory is resolved on every call to `_read_versioned_config` instead of cached at module import, so LaunchAgent crons that export `NEXO_HOME` via a wrapper script reach the right `~/.nexo/config/`. The `Idempotency-Key` header accepts a caller-provided value so application-level retries reuse the same dedup key and the proxy treats them as duplicates instead of new billable requests. Override mode is strict about its bearer source: if `auth_provider.json` is absent or its helper fails, the call raises `ClassifierUnavailableError` rather than fall back to the operator's real `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (a leak vector to the custom proxy). The Anthropic SDK env-var fallback (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` -> `X-Api-Key`) is neutralised around the override-mode constructor so a real `sk-ant-...` value is never paired with the proxy URL on the wire. New `tests/test_call_model_raw_overrides_e2e.py` drives the real SDK against a local `http.server` capturing the wire request and asserts on actual headers and body.
v7.9.28: patch release. Adds optional override files at `~/.nexo/config/llm_endpoint.json` and `~/.nexo/config/auth_provider.json` that let third-party orchestrators redirect Brain Anthropic SDK calls and delegate bearer token resolution to a local command (analogous to git's `credential.helper`). Override mode also injects `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` and `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` into every CLI child Brain spawns so headless crons (deep-sleep, evolution, followup-runner, morning-agent, email-monitor) hit the proxy too &mdash; LaunchAgent processes do not inherit env from a UI process. Idempotency-Key (UUID4 hex) is attached per request for proxy-side dedup of transparent retries. Brain libre standalone (no override files) hits api.anthropic.com directly with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` exactly as before. Validation: 28 dedicated tests plus a 179-test wider regression sweep across resonance, semantic-router, semantic-reasoner, agent-runner, run-automation-prompt, bare-mode, and auto-update-relocate-resonance.
v7.9.27: patch release. Fixes a server-startup hang where the synchronous `task.owner` backfill loaded the multi-minute `LocalZeroShotClassifier` and exceeded the 60s subprocess gate, leaving every restart looping over the same legacy rows and breaking the MCP `initialize` handshake. Startup now invokes `backfill_task_owner.py --rules-only`, completing in milliseconds; Deep Sleep / cron can later re-run without the flag to refine ambiguous `'shared'` rows. Validation: targeted MCP handshake test on a populated runtime, py_compile on patched modules, full pre-release pytest.
v7.9.26: patch release. Centralizes the operator-language contract in headless automation so reports, diaries, syntheses, followups, escalations, and Deep Sleep-generated memory text follow calibration even when templates are written in English. Validation: operator-language, agent-runner, bare-mode, and core-prompt regression tests.
v7.9.25: patch release. Aligns managed Claude Code and Codex bootstraps around the configured user-facing assistant identity, continuity checks before denying prior work, professional autonomy, safety boundaries, and calm user-facing tone. Validation: bootstrap/client-sync tests, Desktop bootstrap test, temp HOME clients sync, and full pre-release pytest.
v7.9.24: patch release. Resolves Desktop lifecycle alias drift after fallback or live diary writes: Brain now stores fallback diary evidence under the registered NEXO SID when available, and stop-nexo-session resolves orphan aliases back to the real active session before stopping. Validation: lifecycle regression tests and ruff.
v7.9.23: patch release. Enriches Desktop lifecycle fallback diaries from continuity snapshots when lifecycle events carry only minimal metadata, preserving recent turn context in the emergency session_diary instead of a generic close note. Validation: lifecycle regression tests, ruff, and local NEXO Desktop 0.28.18 app-exit test with five real messages.
v7.9.22: patch release. Adds a Brain-side emergency fallback diary path for Desktop lifecycle shutdowns: if close/archive/app-exit cannot get a live agent response to the injected diary prompt, Brain writes a desktop-lifecycle-fallback session_diary from the lifecycle payload snapshot instead of losing the shutdown context. Validation: lifecycle regression tests, ruff, release artifact sync, and coordinated Desktop 0.28.17 lifecycle checks.
v7.9.21: patch release. Hardens LaunchAgent repair for macOS already-loaded races discovered during installed-runtime validation: repair boots out by label and plist path, falls back to legacy load, and only treats Bootstrap failed: 5 as success when launchd confirms the expected plist is already loaded. Validation: targeted runtime_power, doctor, update reload tests plus release artifact sync.
v7.9.20: patch release. Hardens packaged update self-heal for real installs: doctor repair now finds runtime/crons/sync.py, LaunchAgent PATH includes the managed Claude runtime under ~/.nexo/runtime/bootstrap/npm-global/bin, runtime root backfill includes claude_cli.py, and Immune skips the optional legacy ~/.claude-mem/claude-mem.db when absent. Validation: targeted pytest, ruff, release artifact sync, and packaged update/doctor regression tests.
v7.9.19: patch release. Cleans runtime doctor/product-health false reds found during final Brain audit: active-task protocol debt is reported as pending work instead of broken install state, interactive Desktop sessions are excluded from automation telemetry scoring, stale filesystem skill rows are pruned, missing executable skill definitions downgrade to guide mode, stale protocol debt drains set `status='resolved'`, and watchdog treats SIGTERM 143 as supervisor reload/interruption. Validation: targeted pytest, ruff, source runtime doctor, watchdog syntax/run, release-readiness, root npm pack, OpenClaw build/test/pack, and installed runtime doctor.
v7.9.18: patch release. Fixes packaged client-sync bootstrap imports when NEXO_HOME is unset, restoring `nexo clients sync`, packaged update client-sync checks, and doctor bootstrap checks. Includes the v7.9.17 Bandit gate fix and the v7.9.16 restart-marker deadlock fix. Validation: bootstrap import regression test, clients-sync shared config test, release-readiness, Bandit high/high, root npm pack, and OpenClaw build/test/pack.
v7.9.17: patch release. Keeps the high-severity Bandit CI gate green by marking the continuity snapshot SHA-1 digest as non-security idempotency usage while preserving stable keys. Includes the v7.9.16 restart-marker deadlock fix. Validation: full pytest suite, release-readiness, Bandit high/high, root npm pack, and OpenClaw build/test/pack.
v7.9.16: patch release. Fixes the MCP restart-required marker deadlock after Brain updates: markers now include only active interactive clients, `nexo_startup` stays reachable during restart recovery, and identified clients auto-ack after restart when installed and process versions match. Also keeps release artifacts and public surfaces aligned before npm publication. Validation: restart marker regression tests, release-readiness, release artifact sync, and OpenClaw build/test.
v7.9.15: patch release. Registers the semantic reasoner caller, persists cortex decision critique metadata for high-stakes decisions, and pins local model choices through a manifest-backed resolver.
v7.9.14: patch release. Hardens G1 task closure so `done` is blocked when verify evidence is trivial, the required change log cannot be persisted, or a high-stakes action lacks a persisted cortex evaluation; repeated failed close attempts now dedupe the same open protocol debt instead of multiplying it. Daily self-audit now runs the protocol-debt stale drainer inline so old debt gets auto-resolved without waiting for a perfect Deep Sleep night, and Codex session parity now flags partial bootstrap/startup/heartbeat drift instead of treating “1 good session” as fully healthy. Validation: pytest -q tests/test_protocol.py tests/test_self_audit.py tests/test_doctor.py (166 passed, 2 xpassed) + pytest -q tests/test_phase_protocol_debt_drain.py (8 passed) + npm pack --dry-run.
v7.9.11: patch release. Canonical Desktop lifecycle completion no longer treats diary confirmation as enough: Brain now requires both diary evidence and real stop evidence before archive/delete/app-exit are considered done. Canonical plans bump to v4 and add wait_for_stop after stop_session, and Brain exposes wait-for-stop plus direct stop-nexo-session surfaces so Desktop can close the exact linked NEXO SID instead of leaving duplicate live sessions behind a reopened conversation. Validation: targeted pytest tests/test_lifecycle_events.py tests/test_doctor.py tests/test_tool_compat_aliases.py tests/test_nexo_brain_onboarding_cli.py tests/test_packaged_update_runtime.py tests/test_continuity_runtime.py tests/test_auto_update_f06_shims.py tests/test_startup_preflight.py (195 passing) plus coordinated Desktop npm test (744 pass, 0 fail, 1 skipped).
v7.9.6: patch release. Closes continuity + MCP restart stability for installed users. Brain now persists conversation-scoped continuity snapshots and resume bundles, activates packaged runtimes atomically under ~/.nexo/core/current, writes a durable mcp-restart-required marker on real version changes, and self-drains old MCP processes until the client restarts against the installed runtime. Coordinated Desktop v0.28.7 uses the same contract so existing brain-only and brain+desktop installs reroute to the new runtime instead of serving mixed old/new MCP code. Validation: pytest tests/test_continuity_runtime.py tests/test_server_protocol_exports.py tests/test_startup_preflight.py tests/test_runtime_update_contract.py (27 passing) and Desktop npm test (727 pass, 0 fail, 1 skipped).
v7.9.5: patch release. Fixes canonical diary confirmation for NEXO Desktop by resolving Desktop/Claude session UUIDs through session_claude_aliases and sessions before checking session_diary. This lets archive/delete/app-exit confirm diaries written by nexo_session_diary_write under the active nexo-* SID instead of timing out on the Desktop UUID. Validation: pytest tests/test_lifecycle_events.py (28 passing) and coordinated Desktop v0.28.6 shutdown/archive/delete/app-exit checks.
v7.9.4: patch release. Blocks the Brain 7.9.3 + Desktop 0.28.2 diary regression by requiring real session_diary evidence before stop_session or archive/delete/close/app-exit completion. canonical_actions now include wait_for_diary_write and Brain rejects canonical completion without diary evidence. Also fixes npm onboarding so --version/subcommands do not launch the wizard with valid legacy/v2 calibration, commits calibration.json atomically after setup completes, and adds nexo-brain warmup-models to predownload the pinned mDeBERTa classifier, BGE embeddings, and fastembed reranker during install/update. Validation: full Brain pytest 2189 passed, release-readiness, npm pack dry-run, and coordinated Desktop v0.28.3 check.
v7.9.3: patch release. Hardens the canonical lifecycle plan shape used by NEXO Desktop close/archive/delete/app-exit diary flows. canonical_actions now publish the v2 canonical contract with type and payload.prompt while keeping one-release compatibility mirrors kind and top-level prompt for older Desktop clients. This lets Desktop execute resume_session -> inject_prompt -> stop_session with one exact owner per lifecycle event and preserve Brain-side dedupe by event_id. Targeted validation: pytest tests/test_lifecycle_events.py (25 passing), release artifact sync, and release-readiness.
v7.9.2: patch release. Completes the Brain semantic-router site migration. Remaining semantic decision callers now route by named decision_kind instead of owning local classifier/LLM policy in each file: r20_constant_change, r34_identity_coherence, t4_r15, t4_r23e, t4_r23f, t4_r23h, followup_operator_attention, drive_signal_type, drive_area, reply_event_type, query_intent and sentiment_intent. Brain's router now centrally owns model choice, thresholds and fallback behaviour for these decisions while structural fallbacks remain in degraded paths. Fixes the headless Guardian map-loading bug found in runtime audit: enforcement_engine._load_map() and agent_runner._build_enforcement_system_prompt() now check the installed core directory (Path(__file__).parent / tool-enforcement-map.json), so packaged jobs launched from ~/.nexo/core load the enforcement map instead of logging "No enforcement map found". Adds tests for every migrated call site and the installed-core map path. Targeted validation: 100 semantic/router/enforcer tests, 125 Drive/cognitive/productization tests, and release-readiness passing.
v7.9.1: patch release. First semantic-router site-migration batch: session_end_intent, r14_correction_learning, r16_declared_done, r17_promise_debt, autonomy_mandate and guard_verbal_ack now call semantic_router.route with named decision_kind policies instead of importing enforcement_classifier directly. Router fast_local and reasoner multipass_local now classify the live context text when present, not the static prompt template, and the migrated callers use semantic labels instead of generic yes/no. Existing fail-closed behaviour and test seams preserved. 105 targeted tests passing. Remaining site migrations stay tracked under NF-SEMANTIC-ROUTER-SITE-MIGRATION. No Desktop bump.
v7.9.0: minor release. Ships the foundation of the semantic stack (router + reasoner + CLI) under the ONEPASS LLM Coverage plan, plus two product-bug fixes observed in the wild on 2026-04-23. New src/semantic_router.py exposes 18 named decision_kinds (13 textual + 5 code-aware) with a per-kind policy table and the layer chain fast_local → semantic_reasoner → remote_fallback. New src/semantic_reasoner.py adds Mode A (multipass_local: reuses the mDeBERTa pin with three prompt-perturbed passes + majority vote + 0.75 floor) and Mode B (cached_llm: wrapper over call_model_raw with a pid+uuid atomic-write 24h-TTL disk cache at ~/.nexo/runtime/operations/semantic-reasoner-cache.json, SHA-256 keyed by decision_kind+normalized input, LRU 2000 entries, corrupt entries dropped). New scripts/semantic-classify.py JSON-in JSON-out CLI lets external MCP clients (including the closed-source NEXO Desktop) query Brain as the single semantic authority. New NEXO_SEMANTIC_REASONER kill switch (0/off/false/no/disable/disabled) honours the plan mandate for a runtime opt-out separate from NEXO_LOCAL_CLASSIFIER. Bug fixes: bin/nexo-brain.js upgrade flow now copies templates/ root the same way fresh install and same-version refresh already did (Maria iMac 7.1.10→7.8.1 had lost 27 core-prompts templates); and tool-enforcement-map.json nexo_startup inject_prompt now instructs the model to preload the 13 mcp__nexo__* protocol tools via ToolSearch before calling nexo_startup when the host defers tool schemas (Claude Code with many MCPs installed). Audit-driven hardening: router/reasoner defensively use getattr over the call_model_raw module and add a trailing except Exception so provider errors degrade with remote_error instead of propagating; cache writes use pid+uuid tmp + fsync + os.replace to survive concurrent writers; NEXO_SEMANTIC_REASONER_TTL parse tolerates malformed values. Tests: +50 (22 router, 20 reasoner, 8 CLI). Per-site migration of existing callers (session_end_intent, r14, r16, r17, r20, r34, T4 gates, tools_drive, nexo-followup-runner) is explicitly deferred to follow-up patch releases and tracked as NF-SEMANTIC-ROUTER-SITE-MIGRATION; nothing in this release changes the behaviour of the existing callers. Companion coordinated release: NEXO Desktop v0.28.0.
v7.8.2: patch release. hook_runs sid observability for compact hooks. New src/hooks/compact_session_resolver.resolve_nexo_sid walks sessions → aliases → per-conv sidecar → legacy global sidecar. pre_compact.py and post_compact.py now store the resolved NEXO sid in hook_runs.session_id (ending the 7/8 empty-row rate) plus claude_session_id + sid_source in metadata. Nine new tests in tests/test_hook_runs_compact_sid_resolution.py pin the five resolver rails, malformed-sidecar rejection, both wrapper paths, and the empty-state fallback. No Desktop bump.
v7.8.1: patch release. pre-compact.sh Layer 2 emergency auto-diary + Layer 3 record_auto_flush now use exact TARGET_SID (resolved from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID), not "latest active session". Fail-closed when no SID resolves. last_diary_ts scoped by session_id. Fixed a bash-escape bug where double-quotes inside a Python comment silently closed the python3 -c argument. New behavioural tests drive the real shell against a fixture DB with two sessions. Pytest 2092 passing. No Desktop bump.
v7.8.0: minor release. Compaction continuity closed end-to-end. New src/hooks/post_compact.py canonical wrapper — PostCompact is now a real registered hook (canonical set grows 8 → 9). pre-compact.sh resolves NEXO SID from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID via sessions.claude_session_id + session_claude_aliases (no more LATEST_SID fallback that broke multi-conv Desktop). Sidecar moves /tmp → $NEXO_HOME/runtime/data/compacting-sid.txt (no cross-conv race). post-compact.sh removes its latest-checkpoint fallback: fail-closed with SID-mismatch diagnostic instead of restoring another conversation. Engine _consume_pending_hook_events() drains NDJSON events emitted by both hooks on every periodic tick so on_event pre_compaction / post_compaction rules fire from the live stream. 11 new invariants in tests/test_v78_compaction_continuity.py. Pytest 2086 passing (+16 vs v7.7). No Desktop bump.
v7.7.0: minor release. Pass 2 of constructor-guardian-90. Closes the six gaps left partial after v7.6. Gap 1: autonomous detector for multi_step_task_detected (heuristic in on_tool_call fires the event after 3 edit/exec/delegate calls without skill_match). Gap 2: R16 classifier prompt recognises the full done-claim vocabulary (sent/delivered/published/deployed/released/fixed/resolved/merged/pushed + Spanish). Gap 3: R_CATALOG extends to plain Edit/Write into artefact-bearing paths; discovery set grows to 8 (adds nexo_personal_scripts_list + nexo_plugin_list). Gap 4: new R_PRIMITIVE_CHOICE runtime rule gates new-artefact writes without a primitive-choice probe. Gap 5: guardian_default v1.5.0 raises R11_plugin_load_pre_inventory soft→hard and adds R_PRIMITIVE_CHOICE at soft. Gap 6: tests/test_v77_enforcement_gaps.py pins 12 invariants across all six rails. Pytest 2070 passing (+14). Companion: NEXO Desktop v0.27.0.
v7.6.0: minor release. Closes drift between tool-enforcement-map.json v2.2 and the two enforcement engines. Brain engine now dispatches before_tool, on_event, and conditional alongside the previously-handled types — before v7.6 these three were declared in the map but only Desktop honoured them. after_tool dependencies now satisfy per-instance (monotonic _tool_instance_counter) instead of once-per-session. Map v2.2 tightens two triggers: nexo_learning_add grace_messages 3 → 0, and nexo_task_open conditional threshold 10 → 4 with level must + explicit inject_prompt. guardian_default.json v1.4.0 hardens R15/R17/R22/R_CATALOG from soft to hard, and raises R34_identity_coherence from shadow to soft. New contract test test_v76_map_parity.py pins six invariants including Brain↔Desktop dispatch parity. Companion release: NEXO Desktop v0.26.0 mirrors the fixes + default hardening. Pytest: 2056 passing.
v7.5.0: minor release. Promotes nexo_lifecycle_event from ledger + reconciliation authority to canonical authority of session-end. Brain now owns the prompt, sequence, and timing of diary+stop; Desktop v0.25.0 (closed-source companion) is the conduit that executes Brain's plan. New 2-call contract: nexo_lifecycle_event returns a versioned canonical_plan (resume_session → inject_prompt → stop_session, stable ids, per-action timeouts); nexo_lifecycle_complete_canonical confirms execution with a per-action results array. No polling. canonical_plan_id is deterministic (sha256 of event_id + plan_version, truncated to 24 chars). Migration m52 extends lifecycle_events with six canonical_* columns + an index; pre-v7.5 rows carry NULL. session_diary dedupes re-delivery: if Desktop crashes between inject and confirm, the next nexo_lifecycle_event for the same event_id inspects session_diary for rows written after canonical_dispatched_at and short-circuits to already_processed. Seven explicit delivery_status values (accepted / processed / canonical_pending / canonical_done / already_processed / retryable_error / rejected). switch and window-close remain observational (no plan ever issued). nexo lifecycle record exit code 0 now covers canonical_pending. 24/24 lifecycle tests green; 2050 total pytest green (10 pre-existing unrelated failures). MCP tool count 262 → 263.
v7.4.1: patch release. Honest correction of v7.4.0's role in the guardian-claude-desktop-plan.md pipeline. nexo_lifecycle_event is a ledger + reconciliation authority, NOT the canonical executor of diary+stop (that injection still lives in Desktop's closeConversationGraceful; moving it to Brain requires a Brain↔Claude-session bridge that is scheduled for v7.5). Also declares explicitly that v7.4.0 wired only close/delete/archive through the service; switch/window-close/app-exit ship in Desktop v0.24.1. No schema changes; VALID_ACTIONS stays at six entries frozen by a contract test.
v7.4.0: minor release. Brain-side first slice of guardian-claude-desktop-plan.md. Ships nexo_lifecycle_event + nexo_lifecycle_status MCP tools and the lifecycle_events table (migration m51) so NEXO Desktop v0.24.0 can persist close/delete/archive transitions durably with idempotency by event_id. Adds nexo lifecycle CLI subcommand. 12 new contract tests. No breaking changes. Total MCP tools 260 → 262. The paired Desktop v0.24.1 wires the remaining switch/window-close/app-exit routes; canonical diary+stop execution inside the handler is deferred to v7.5.
v7.3.0: hotfix minor release over v7.2.0. Corrects three critical bugs that surfaced right after v7.2.0 went live. B11 Guardian wire: the new `src/hooks/pre_tool_use.py` entrypoint is now registered in `src/hooks/manifest.json` and `hooks/hooks.json` so the Claude Code PreToolUse event actually reaches Guardian — before this, `guardian-runtime-overrides.json` in hard mode was silently inert for G3-destructive, G3-SSH and G4-guard_check gates. A parallel SSH prescreen in `hook_guardrails.process_pre_tool_event` forces `op=write` on remote-write patterns the local shell classifier could not see. B10 post-sync: `_run_post_install_hooks_fresh(dest, env)` invokes each whitelisted hook (`_persist_guardian_hard_defaults`, `_maybe_promote_adaptive_weights_empirically`) in a clean subprocess against the newly copied tree, so the first `nexo update` that introduces a new post-install hook actually runs it — previously the hook dispatch used the pre-upgrade module held in memory and silently no-op'd. B12 map distribution: `tool-enforcement-map.json` is now part of the npm `files` whitelist so fresh `npm install -g nexo-brain` ships it, closing the three-way gap (Brain npm + Desktop bundle + runtime sync) that prevented Desktop from ever discovering the map on new installs. Also ships PE1 rapid items: 5 new `destructive_command` preset entries in `src/presets/entities_universal.json` (coverage floor 7→12) and the `guardian-metrics` daily cron at 02:15. Desktop v0.23.0 ships the renderer-side half of B12 (bundle + preflight + visible warning) plus B13/B14 (the "Parar → Enviar cola" recovery loop).
v7.2.0: minor release consolidating three parallel workstreams into a single Guardian-active-by-default train. Block K closure: G1 enforcer active (PostToolUse nudge when `task_open` returned `mode ∈ {defer, ask, verify}` without the paired `next_action`), G3 SSH remote-write detector (catches `ssh host "cat > ..."`, `scp upload`, `rsync upload`, `sftp -b batch` and 10+ other patterns the local destructive gate never saw), new `guardian_runtime_config.py` resolver unifying gate modes (env > `guardian-runtime-overrides.json` > default), `_persist_guardian_hard_defaults` so every non-ephemeral install gets Guardian hard automatically on the next `nexo update`, adaptive-weights empirical promotion (shadow→learned on ≥200 samples + ≥2 days, auto-promoted during update). F0.6 hardening: `nexo rollback f06` CLI, `prune_runtime_backups.py` promoted to core, authoritative `docs/f06-layout-contract.md`, three new doctor boot-tier checks (`check_core_dev_packaged_install`, `check_dashboard_desktop_contract`, `check_f06_migration_consistency`), `scripts/nexo-migrate-nora.sh` + `scripts/f0-safe-apply-remote.sh` idempotent migration, v6.x→F0.6 rollback hardening tests. Small fixes: R34 bool(`"unknown"`) force-inject corrected, `classify_scripts_dir` dedup by realpath, module-level path constants lazy-evaluated in `public_contribution.py` / `tools_sessions.py` / `plugins/recover.py` / `plugins/update.py`, schedule override audit log at `runtime/logs/core-schedule-overrides.log`, `scripts/pre-release-verify.sh` + `docs/release-discipline.md`, pre-commit hook for `tool-enforcement-map.json` drift. Desktop v0.22.6 remains compatible (MCP external contract unchanged).
v7.1.10: follow-up over v7.1.8. Ships two rescue batches of WIP that were stashed aside during the v7.1.8 release window. First rescue: `src/autonomy_mandate.py` expands the mandate-detection vocabulary (hazlo todo / no pares / estás al mando / te dejo al mando / sigue sin parar / haz el plan completo), adds three honest flags on MandateState (execute_until_blocker, suppress_mid_task_menus, revalidate_after_compaction) with session filtering, wires post/pre-compact hooks that read those flags, surfaces them through protocol/workflow handlers and session payload, and introduces the new `src/checkpoint_policy.py` module with tests. Second rescue: `scripts/verify_release_readiness.py` gains a smoke-artifact contract pass that validates `release-contracts/smoke/v<version>.json` before any tag push, the release-final audit skill references the new contract, `src/hook_guardrails.py` + `src/hooks/post_tool_use.py` refine the post-tool protocol reminder path with a new contract test, and a couple of core prompts (task-close evidence, r14 correction learning) get wording polish. Both rescues are ortogonal to v7.1.8 and fully covered by tests.
v7.1.8: batch release over v7.1.7. Consolidates the Block K Guardian/Enforcer roadmap (G2 auto-drain of stale protocol_debt rows, G3 destructive-command pre-tool gate, G4 guard_check-required gate, G7 inline guard ack on task_open, G8 Guardian Health section in the session briefing), Block D hardcode cleanup (backfill_task_owner routes intent through the local zero-shot classifier, physical supersede of the duplicate NEXO-product learning pair via migration v50, semantic-hardcodes audit script), and Block E product guards (hook rejects agentic LaunchAgent plist edits, auto_update agent-name fallbacks route through DEFAULT_ASSISTANT_NAME, email_config stops exporting `francisco_emails`, runner-health-check and nexo_personal_automation promoted from personal to core). Also: `nexo chat` surfaces every installed terminal client, `add_email_account` pins its read-modify-write with `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`, `check_no_personal_data.sh` gains regex-shape detection.
v7.1.7: patch release over v7.1.6. Operator-facing email automation now respects the calibrated operator language end-to-end: `email-monitor` carries that language into its prompt contract and direct `needs_interactive` escalation emails stop falling back to English on Spanish setups.
v7.1.6: patch release over v7.1.5. Structural core crons now have dedicated cadence overrides via `schedule-overrides.json` plus CLI/MCP surfaces, Desktop can edit them from a dedicated Core schedules tab, explicit-home Desktop detection stays isolated from global install markers, and the shipped `synthesis` launchagent template/docs are back in sync with the live 06:00 daily manifest.
v7.1.5: patch release over v7.1.4. Standalone catchup/runtime-root paths now lazy-load the interactive automation helpers, explicit-home Desktop detection ignores the operator machine's global app install, and legacy `nexo_doctor` script callers keep working without an explicit `plane`. This keeps maintenance/release flows stable while the public surfaces stay aligned with the coordinated Desktop line.
v7.1.1: hotfix over v7.1.0. `nexo update` now distinguishes packaged installs from true source-linked runtimes instead of treating `~/.nexo/core` as a mutable repo, the runtime sync path is shim-aware when replacing `db`, `cognitive`, `skills-core`, root Python modules, and other F0.6 compatibility links, and the local-classifier auto-install now uses the same Python interpreter that runs NEXO instead of a generic `pip3`. Result: packaged installs update cleanly again, while real source-sync installs keep working against recorded repos without `FileExistsError`, same-file copy crashes, or cross-interpreter classifier installs.
v7.1.0: closes the post-F0.6 product/runtime contract. `~/.nexo/core` is now treated as the canonical shipped code root across install/update/sync, layout healers re-run automatically, Desktop consumes `guardian-runtime-surfaces.json` generated by Brain instead of stale manual lists, `email-monitor` / `followup-runner` / `morning-agent` are first-class configurable core automations, the email runtime model now distinguishes agent-vs-operator inboxes with permissions and fallback routing, and the local classifier baseline auto-installs on fresh installs and `nexo update` unless the operator sets `NEXO_LOCAL_CLASSIFIER=off`.
v7.0.1: hotfix over v7.0.0. `src/db/_core.py::DB_PATH` was the only caller still hardcoded to the legacy pre-F0.6 path `~/.nexo/data/nexo.db`. Every command that opened the process-wide DB connection (`nexo email list`, `nexo scripts list`, `nexo_task_open`, ...) silently returned empty results despite the real table being populated. Fix: DB_PATH is now transition-aware. NEXO_TEST_DB and NEXO_DB env overrides keep priority.

v7.0.0: BREAKING — Plan Consolidado fase F0.6. Physical separation of the runtime tree into `~/.nexo/{core,personal,runtime}/`. The flat layout (`~/.nexo/scripts/`, `brain/`, `data/`, `operations/`, ...) is gone. Operators on v6.x are auto-migrated on first `nexo update` to v7.0.0; fresh installs land directly in the new tree. New `paths.py` module centralises every runtime path helper. 24 src files refactored, 7 shell scripts updated, 71 `personal_scripts.path` rows UPDATEd transactionally, 40 LaunchAgent plists rewritten. 1551 tests pass. The companion NEXO Desktop release (v0.21.0) updates its hardcoded paths so auto-update keeps working.

v6.5.0: Plan Consolidado fase F0.2. New CLI verbs `nexo scripts enable|disable|status <name>` (all accept `--json`). The cron wrapper (`nexo-cron-wrapper.sh`) reads the `personal_scripts.enabled` flag on every tick and short-circuits to `exit 0` with `summary='[disabled]'` when the operator has the script off — the LaunchAgent stays loaded so re-enabling is a single CLI call. The upsert path on `personal_scripts` no longer overwrites `enabled` on the UPDATE branch, so the operator-set flag is now sticky across `nexo scripts sync` runs. New helper functions `set_personal_script_enabled` + `get_personal_script_status`. 4 new tests in `tests/test_personal_scripts_enabled.py`. The companion NEXO Desktop client (closed-source, distributed separately) wires the same toggle into its Settings → Automatizaciones panel.

v6.4.0: Plan Consolidado fase F1. New `email_accounts` table (m46) stores multi-tenant mailbox config with passwords pointed at the existing `credentials` table (never duplicated). New CLI `nexo email setup` (interactive wizard for first-time operators) + `nexo email add --label X --email X --imap-host X --password-stdin --json` (non-interactive, used by NEXO Desktop v0.19.0 Email panel — passwords pipe through stdin so they never appear on argv). `list`/`test`/`remove` accept `--json` for machine consumers. `email_config.load_email_config()` is the single loader (DB first, legacy `~/.nexo/nexo-email/config.json` fallback). Auto-migrator runs on next session (`auto_update.py`) — existing operators upgrade transparently. `nexo-email-monitor.py` and `nexo-send-reply.py` now go through the new loader. 8 tests in `tests/test_email_accounts.py`. Bandit-clean (`_debt_fingerprint` SHA1 marked `usedforsecurity=False`).

v6.3.1: privacy hotfix — remove operator-specific vhost_mapping entries that v6.3.0 accidentally shipped inside the public `entities_universal.json`. Operator private entries now live in `~/.nexo/brain/presets/entities_local.json` (`.gitignore`'d, seeded from `entities_local.sample.json`).

v6.3.0: Plan Consolidado wave 2, coordinated with NEXO Desktop v0.18.0. Extended `cognitive_sentiment` shape (`is_correction`, `valence`, `intent` enum); entities schema gains `aliases`/`metadata`/`source`/`confidence`/`access_mode` via idempotent migration m44; 21 labelled rule fixtures + R13 spike gates (FP < 5%, P95 < 3s); `artifact_class` preset entries (`shopify_banner_block`, `changelog_entry`, `email_to_operator_contact`); R34 identity-coherence added to the system prompt with an explicit anti-example; Fase F telemetry loops (`fase_f_loops` + Deep Sleep `phase_guardian_analysis` report); pinned local zero-shot classifier skeleton (`MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli` at revision `a1a5a76a8cb44edb4f92e7e2ea4f0e0ce8ce6e97`) plus `docs/classifier-model-notes.md`; `process_pre_tool_event` respects `NEXO_MIGRATING=1`; `origin` column on `personal_scripts` via m45; T4 LLM gate wraps R15/R23e/R23f/R23h with byte-for-byte Py ↔ JS parity. `enforcement_classifier.classify` now supports `tristate=True` so an unparseable conservative fallback returns `"unknown"` instead of silently suppressing destructive rules.

v6.1.1: small fix — `nexo --help` refreshes `Latest: vX` via npm even when invoked via subprocess with piped stdio. Prior TTY gate in `_should_refresh_latest_version()` blocked NEXO Desktop's subprocess from ever populating the version cache, so the Brain auto-update banner never saw a newer version and users had to run `nexo update` manually. Fix removes the TTY gate; 6-hour `_load_latest_version_cache` stays as the real rate-limit; fail-closes to installed-only when npm unreachable.

v6.1.0: Protocol Enforcer Fase 2 ships. Capa 2 runtime guardian with 25 rules (R13 pre-Edit guard, R14 post-correction window, R16 declared-done, R25 Nora/María read-only block, plus 21 more across stream wrapper layers C/D/D2). `src/enforcement_engine.py` (Python headless) + `enforcement-engine.js` (NEXO Desktop) byte-for-byte template parity. Companion migration v43 `session_claude_aliases` fixes NEXO Desktop multi-conversation edits blocked with "unknown target": each Claude spawn's SessionStart hook now registers an alias so `_resolve_nexo_sid` resolves every conversation's UUID to the same NEXO sid. External-LLM audit + Opus 4.7 self-audit cycle applied: log redaction covers Bearer/sk-/pk-/api_key/JWT/AWS/GitHub/Shopify/KEY=VALUE/mysql -p<pass>, R23f heredoc multiline, R23h native PATH resolution (no shell injection), R14 detector awaited, cross-engine parity harness strict mode. Red-team suite (32 attempts across 12 rules). Guardian telemetry NDJSON + `scripts/install_guardian.py` installer + `docs/guardian-quickstart.md` operator guide. 291 pass + 2 skip (documented: `-F` commit-msg is not `--force`; `--force-with-lease` is safe by spec).

v6.0.6: installer no longer leaks `export PATH="$NEXO_HOME/bin:$PATH"` into the developer's real shell profile when `NEXO_HOME` is non-canonical. `src/auto_update.py::_ensure_runtime_cli_in_shell()` and both `bin/nexo-brain.js` call sites (install Step 8 + migration path) now consult `_should_skip_shell_profile_backfill()` / `shouldSkipShellProfileBackfill()`. Fresh installs at `$HOME/.nexo` are unaffected; operators can force-skip via `NEXO_SKIP_SHELL_PROFILE=1`. Root cause: the rc-file list was computed from `Path.home()` / `os.homedir()` regardless of `NEXO_HOME`, so any pytest run with `NEXO_HOME=/tmp/pytest-xxx` polluted `~/.bash_profile` / `~/.bashrc` / `~/.zshrc` on the developer's real machine. Five new regression tests in `tests/test_auto_update_shell_profile.py`. CI gate `tests.yml` introduced in v6.0.5 catches this category of drift. Also cleans a duplicate `.github/workflows/tests 2.yml` committed by accident. 1098 passed, 2 xfailed, 1 skipped.
v6.0.4: interactive launchers now honour `preferences.default_resonance`. Pre-v6.0.4 both `nexo chat` (`build_interactive_client_command`) and the dashboard's "Open followup in Terminal" action (`build_followup_terminal_shell_command`) picked `--model` / `--effort` straight from `config/schedule.json`'s `client_runtime_profiles`, so users who switched their Resonance in NEXO Desktop Preferences (Alto → writes `brain/calibration.json`) kept getting the stale tier cached in the legacy profile (usually `max`). Headless runs (`run_automation_prompt`) and NEXO Desktop new-sessions already honoured the preference correctly; only the two terminal launchers were stuck. New `_resolve_interactive_model_and_effort(caller, backend, ...)` helper consults `resonance_map.resolve_model_and_effort` first and falls back to `client_runtime_profiles` only when the resonance contract is missing. `run_automation_interactive` propagates `caller=` and `tier=` into the builder; `nexo_followup_terminal` joins `nexo_chat` / `desktop_new_session` / `nexo_update_interactive` in `USER_FACING_CALLERS` so the dashboard launcher resolves against the user's preference. 8 tests updated, full suite green.
v6.0.3: double-fix release. (1) `resonance_tiers.json` now lives at the public contract path `~/.nexo/brain/resonance_tiers.json` on install and update; pre-v6.0.3 it was written to `~/.nexo/` and NEXO Desktop ≥ 0.12.0 failed to start Claude with *"NEXO Brain contract missing"* on every fresh install and every update from 6.0.0 / 6.0.1 / 6.0.2. New `publishBrainContracts()` helper in `bin/nexo-brain.js`, three-step lookup in `src/resonance_map.py` (`NEXO_HOME/brain/` → legacy `NEXO_HOME/` → `src/`), and an idempotent `_relocate_resonance_tiers_contract(dest)` migration in `auto_update.py` that promotes legacy runtimes on the next `nexo update` and unlinks the old copy. (2) `nexo_guard_check` now writes the caller's SID into `guard_checks.session_id` instead of the empty string that slipped in in v6.0.2. Resolution walks `NEXO_SID` env → `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` env translated via `sessions.external_session_id` → most-recently-updated `sessions` row. Strict-protocol sessions stop tripping *"no guard_check seen for this session"* right after a successful call. 9 new pytest cases, full suite stays green.
v6.0.2: reserved caller prefix `personal/` for user-owned scripts outside the NEXO Brain repo. Any script living in `~/.nexo/scripts/` can now invoke the automation backend with its own caller id (`caller="personal/<descriptive-id>"`) and pick a resonance tier (`tier="maximo"|"alto"|"medio"|"bajo"`) without editing `src/resonance_map.py`. Precedence for personal callers: explicit `tier=` → explicit `reasoning_effort=` → `calibration.preferences.default_resonance` → `DEFAULT_RESONANCE` ("alto"). Invalid tier values fall through silently, never crash. New kwarg `tier` on `run_automation_prompt`, `run_automation_interactive`, `nexo_helper.run_automation_text`, `nexo_helper.run_automation_json`; `--tier` / `--caller` flags on `nexo-agent-run.py`. New guide `docs/personal-scripts-guide.md` for any NEXO session helping an operator author a personal script. Registered callers (`USER_FACING_CALLERS` / `SYSTEM_OWNED_CALLERS`) keep v6.0.0 behaviour; non-`personal/` callers without a registry entry still raise `UnregisteredCallerError`. 3 new pytest modules (14 cases), suite stays green (1079 passed).
v6.0.1: hotfix on top of 6.0.0. `protocol_settings.py` now treats the process as interactive when either stdin+stdout are TTYs or `NEXO_INTERACTIVE=1` is exported — NEXO Desktop 0.12.0 spawns `claude` through pipes and fell back to `lenient` in v6.0.0. Truthy-looking values (`true`/`yes`/`on`) are rejected; only the literal `"1"` opts in. The `PostToolUse` hook gains an inbox-autodetect stage that emits a `systemMessage` when the session has unread `nexo_send` messages and 60s+ have passed since the last heartbeat; rate-limited to one reminder per minute per SID via the new `hook_inbox_reminders` table (migration m42). `sessions.last_heartbeat_ts` is stamped by `nexo_heartbeat` on every invocation and read by the hook to decide whether to surface the reminder. `NEXO_INTERACTIVE` is an internal Brain↔Electron contract; it is not user-facing and is not a resurrection of the removed `NEXO_PROTOCOL_STRICTNESS`. 6 new pytest modules, full suite stays green (1065 passed).
v6.0.0: BREAKING. Tier-only setup. Onboarding asks one resonance tier (`maximo`/`alto`/`medio`/`bajo`) and `src/resonance_tiers.json` becomes the single source of truth for the per-backend `(model, effort)` map consumed by `src/resonance_map.py` via `load_resonance_table()`. Legacy `client_runtime_profiles.{claude_code,codex}.{model,reasoning_effort}` purged from `schedule.json` on upgrade. Protocol strictness is no longer configurable — TTY → `strict`, no-TTY → `lenient`; `NEXO_PROTOCOL_STRICTNESS` env, `preferences.protocol_strictness`, and the `default/normal/off/warn/soft` aliases removed. `preferences.show_pending_at_start` moves to NEXO Desktop electron-store. The seven core hooks unified behind `src/hooks/manifest.json` (plugin and npm modes read the same file); two new hooks ship: `Notification` → `hook_observability.record_activity()` and `SubagentStop` → auto-closes stale `protocol_tasks`. `auto_capture.py` wired to `UserPromptSubmit` + `PostToolUse` with persistent 1h dedup (`auto_capture_dedup` table) and automatic `nexo_learning_add` on correction matches. `~/.nexo/hooks_status.json` published after every `registerAllCoreHooks()` for NEXO Desktop ≥0.12.0. New `nexo-brain --skip` flag aliases `--yes`/`--defaults`. `calibration_migration.apply_v6_purge()` invoked once from `auto_update._run_runtime_post_sync()`: never raises, never overwrites an existing `preferences.default_resonance`. Tests: 7 new pytests, 1057 passed, 1 skipped.
v5.10.2: auto-bootstraps `brain/profile.json` from `brain/calibration.json` on `nexo update`. Operators whose onboarding flow was interrupted before v5.9.x had `role` and `technical_level` recorded under `calibration.meta.*` only, with no `profile.json` file — NEXO Desktop's Preferencias → Avanzado tab then rendered an empty `{}` for the profile block with no context. New `_bootstrap_profile_from_calibration_meta(dest)` runs inside `_run_runtime_post_sync()` and seeds `profile.json` with `role` / `technical_level` / `name` / `language` when any are present. Never overwrites a populated profile, never raises, idempotent. Paired with Desktop v0.11.2 which adds explanatory headers to both JSON blocks in the Avanzado tab and renders a friendly placeholder when `profile.json` is still missing. Also fixes `test_user_facing_caller_with_no_user_default_uses_alto` which was leaking the real ~/.nexo state after the v5.10.1 migration. 10 new tests (`test_auto_update_bootstrap_profile.py`). Full suite: 1021 passed, 1 skipped.
v5.10.1: silent, one-shot migration that recovers legacy `reasoning_effort="max"` (and siblings `xhigh`/`high`/`medium`) from `config/schedule.json` into `preferences.default_resonance` in `brain/calibration.json`. Any operator who configured `max` before v5.9.0 and never touched the new selector was silently falling back to `DEFAULT_RESONANCE="alto"` on interactive calls since the v5.10.0 update. New `_migrate_effort_to_resonance(dest)` runs inside `_run_runtime_post_sync()` exactly once: `max→maximo`, `xhigh→alto`, `high→medio`, `medium→bajo`. No-op when calibration/schedule already declares `default_resonance`; idempotent; conservative; never raises. 10 new tests plus a test-harness fix that unblocks `test_cron_recovery.py` against main. Full suite: 1011 passed, 1 skipped.
v5.10.0: extract-path bloat fix + `caller=` enforced + personal scripts on the map. New `bare_mode` kwarg on `run_automation_prompt` wires `claude --bare` for JSON-only callers (skips CLAUDE.md auto-load, hooks, LSP, plugin sync, keychain probe — ~4.3× faster per child, ~9s → ~2s). `deep-sleep/extract` and `deep-sleep/synthesize` auto-enable bare when an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is resolvable (env, `~/.claude/anthropic-api-key.txt`, `~/.nexo/config/anthropic-api-key.txt`); silent fallback to the normal path when no key is available. `caller=` is now **mandatory** — `run_automation_prompt` raises `UnregisteredCallerError` without a registered caller. Five personal scripts joined the map (`personal/email-monitor`, `personal/github-monitor`, `personal/post-x`, `personal/followup-runner`, `personal/orchestrator-v2`). gbp/* marketing callers bumped medio → alto (public-facing copy). 65 legacy protocol debts bulk-resolved. 10 new bare-mode tests (974 total in suite).
v5.9.1: adds `default_resonance` to the Desktop-facing schema exported by `nexo schema --json`. NEXO Desktop's existing Preferences → Preferences tab renders the selector automatically via its `buildFieldsFromBrainSchema()` path — no Desktop release required. Four localised options (`Máximo` / `Alto (recomendado)` / `Medio` / `Bajo` in Spanish, `Maximum` / `High (recommended)` / `Medium` / `Low` in English). New `resonance_map._load_user_default_resonance()` reads `brain/calibration.json` first (new canonical location, where Desktop writes) and falls back to `config/schedule.json` (where the v5.9.0 CLI wrote). `nexo preferences --resonance` now writes to both. `resolve_tier_for_caller` consults the helper when `user_default` is not passed, so `nexo chat` / `launch_interactive_client` pick up the Desktop-edited value with no extra wiring. 6 new tests (26 total in resonance_map suite).
v5.9.0: centralised resonance map + unified automation session log.
